statement: The environment is probably what I’m most passionate about. I constantly try to find new ways to help protect it, and I spend time learning about it too.
I join occasional protest.

It’s something I think about every day. I feel like I already do as much as I can by sharing information on social media, joining protests (e.g. Extinction Rebellion), eating vegan and riding my bike.
I tend to do small, daily actions. I like to lead by example.
I participate in tree plantings, Cleanup Australia, and other local events.
I volunteer regularly with a group of friends in political party campaigning, cleanup events, protests, bushcare and gardening bees.
The environment is probably what I’m most passionate about. I constantly try to find new ways to help protect it, and I spend time learning about it too.
I want to do my best to reduce my impact on the environment.
This generation is not responsible for the situation, I don’t see why we should be sacrificing our comfort for the damage the previous generation knowingly generated.
There is no reason to do much, the situation is already disastrous and there is no perspective of improvement with the current economic system.
My main impact is being a volunteer with Australian Wildlife Conservancy.

I believe science and innovation will resolve these issues, as previous issues have also been resolved. There’s no reason to dramatise.
I don’t do much beyond using the right bin and reducing my user of the car. But I want to do more.

I think the main way we can have an impact is by voting, and campaigning for political parties that care about the environment, like the Greens.
